description:
A utility-scale solar developer is conducting a module technology selection study for a 50 MW (DC) ground-mounted PV project in Daggett, California (34.867°N, -116.783°W). Evaluate three candidate module technologies to determine which delivers the lowest LCOE and best 25-year financial performance using SAM's physics-based PV model and the Daggett weather file.

System Parameters:
- Size: 50,000 kW DC, single-axis tracking, GCR 0.4.
- Inverter efficiency: 98%, DC losses: 8%.

Module Technologies:
- Tech A (Standard mono-Si): 20.4% efficiency, Pmp=440W, Vmp=41.1V, Imp=10.7A, Voc=49.5V, Isc=11.4A, temp coeff Pmp=-0.36%/C, price $1.05/W DC.
- Tech B (Premium HJT): 22.8% efficiency, Pmp=490W, Vmp=43.5V, Imp=11.3A, Voc=52.2V, Isc=12.0A, temp coeff Pmp=-0.26%/C, price $1.20/W DC.
- Tech C (CdTe): 19.1% efficiency, Pmp=440W, Vmp=88.0V, Imp=5.0A, Voc=108V, Isc=5.4A, temp coeff Pmp=-0.28%/C, price $0.95/W DC.

Financial Assumptions:
- BOS: $0.45/W DC, inverter: $0.06/W DC, EPC overhead: 10% of equipment.
- O&M: $14/kW-yr, degradation: 0.5%/yr.
- Analysis: 25 years, 6.0% discount rate, 30% federal ITC.
- Debt: 60% fraction, 5.5% loan rate, 18-year term.
- PPA price: $35/MWh.

Model each technology's individual electrical parameters (efficiency, temperature coefficients, and cell-level I-V characteristics) to determine Year 1 annual energy (MWh), capacity factor (%), total installed cost ($), real LCO
